findings.message: The rename_item route writes item.name directly and commits without recalculating slug, so the slug column diverges from name on every rename. The model documents slug as always-derived-from-name and the repository provides update_item_name() specifically to keep both in sync, but this handler bypasses that helper and even acknowledges the divergence in its own docstring. This produces a persistent consistency violation in the data store: the derived slug (also unique/indexed) becomes stale and can no longer be trusted to match name, and lookups by the canonical slug will resolve to the wrong/old value.
findings.suggestion: Route the rename through the repository helper that already maintains the invariant — call update_item_name(session, item_id, payload.name) (returning 404 when it yields None) instead of mutating item.name inline — so slug = _make_slug(name) is recomputed atomically in the same commit.
